meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't scale projects (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't scale projects. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup to Solve Scaling Issues for Student Apps
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your apps fail to scale. Learn to implement advanced analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to identify bottlenecks and optimize performance.
content.answer_block: To scale your application, you must replace intuition with data. By implementing event-based analytics via Antigravity, you gain granular visibility into user friction points. This setup allows you to identify performance bottlenecks in real-time, enabling surgical code optimizations that transform a stagnant project into a high-performance, production-ready SaaS application.
content.intro:
You’ve built the features, polished the UI, and pushed to production—yet your user growth hit a brick wall. The 'can't scale' syndrome isn't a failure of code; it's a failure of visibility. When you fly blind, you cannot optimize for the bottlenecks slowing your application down.

This tutorial turns the tide. We aren't just logging clicks; we are architecting a telemetry backbone. By integrating Antigravity’s event-stream engine with Claude Code’s autonomous refactoring capabilities, you will build a diagnostic layer that shows you exactly where your app is losing momentum.

steps.title: Define Instrumentation Schema
steps.description: Identify the critical user journey paths (onboarding, conversion, and churn) to ensure your analytics capture meaningful business outcomes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest a JSON-based event schema for tracking user interaction, focusing on performance-sensitive routes and API latency markers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a tracking middleware file based on the Antigravity schema definition provided.'

steps.title: Inject Telemetry Middleware
steps.description: Wrap your core API endpoints in an automated telemetry layer that captures request metadata without polluting business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable middleware function that captures request duration, status codes, and user IDs, then pushes them to the Antigravity endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Inject the telemetry middleware into all routes located in the /api directory.'

steps.title: Implement Performance Heartbeats
steps.description: Client-side telemetry is vital for scaling. Track frontend render times and component mount speeds to identify client-side bottlenecks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a hook or service that calculates the time-to-interactive for our main dashboard and logs it as a performance metric to Antigravity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the performance heartbeat hook into Dashboard.tsx and UserFlow.tsx.'

steps.title: Configure Automated Alerts
steps.description: Set up threshold-based monitoring so you are notified of scaling issues before users experience them.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an Antigravity alert rule that triggers when 5xx error rates exceed 1% or API latency exceeds 500ms for more than 3 consecutive requests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add a configuration file for Antigravity alerts based on the defined error and latency thresholds.'

steps.title: Optimize Based on Data
steps.description: Use the gathered data to perform targeted refactoring on slow-performing database queries or heavy computations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the log data from the last 24 hours to identify the top three slowest database queries and suggest optimized indexing strategies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Refactor the identified slow queries to use optimized indexing or caching strategies as suggested by Antigravity.'

pro_tips: Always sample your analytics data; logging 100% of events at high scale will increase your egress costs and latency.
Tag your events with the commit hash to correlate performance regressions directly to specific code deployments.
Treat analytics code as production code—ensure it has its own unit tests to prevent it from crashing your main application.
Use semantic event naming (e.g., 'user_onboarding_completed') rather than generic labels to make dashboards readable for non-technical stakeholders.
Monitor 'Cold Starts' if you are using serverless architectures, as these are often the silent killers of perceived app performance.
content.next_steps: Once your telemetry is live, spend 48 hours collecting data. Use the 'Optimize Based on Data' step to perform your first A/B test or performance refactor, then document the latency reduction in your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical visibility of a senior engineer. Use this data to prove your app can handle real-world traffic, turning a student project into a professional-grade SaaS. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
